title: NuclearQAv2: A Structured Benchmark for Evaluating Domain-Science Competence in Large Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2606.27047v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of tasks, but ensuring their reliability in highly technical domains remains a significant challenge. In nuclear engineering, problem solving often requires not only factual knowledge but also quantitative reasoning and conceptual understanding. To address the need for systematic evaluation in this domain, we introduce NuclearQAv2, a benchmark for assessing LLMs on nuclear engineering knowledge. The benchmark comprises approximately 1,240 question-answer pairs spanning three categories: boolean, numeric, and verbal. NuclearQAv2 is constructed using a hybrid pipeline that combines expert-authored questions, existing datasets, and LLM-assisted generation from domain-specific technical corpora. By leveraging structured prompting for both automated question generation and response evaluation, the proposed framework enables scalable benchmark construction

Title: NuclearQAv2: A Structured Benchmark for Evaluating Domain-Science Competence in Large Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27047v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of tasks, but ensuring their reliability in highly technical domains remains a significant challenge. translated_title: NuclearQAv2：评估大型语言模型在领域科学能力上的结构化基准测试
translated_abstract: 大型语言模型（LLM）在广泛的任务中展现出强大的性能，但确保其在高度技术性领域的可靠性仍是一项重大挑战。在核工程领域，问题求解不仅需要事实性知识，还需要定量推理和概念理解。为应对该领域系统化评估的需求，我们引入了NuclearQAv2，一个用于评估LLM在核工程知识方面的基准测试。该基准测试包含约1240个问答对，涵盖三类问题：布尔型、数值型和文字型。NuclearQAv2采用混合构建流程，结合了专家撰写的问题、现有数据集以及基于领域特定技术语料库的LLM辅助生成。通过利用结构化提示进行自动问题生成和响应评估，所提出的框架能够实现可扩展的基准测试构建。
tldr: 本文提出了NuclearQAv2基准测试，通过1240个涵盖布尔、数值和文字三类问题的问答对，结合专家、数据集和LLM辅助生成，系统评估大型语言模型在核工程领域的知识掌握与推理能力。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces NuclearQAv2, a benchmark with 1,240 question-answer pairs across boolean, numeric, and verbal categories, built via a hybrid pipeline of expert-authored questions, existing datasets, and LLM-assisted generation, to systematically evaluate large language models' knowledge and reasoning in nuclear engineering.
